QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $707.24 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Tuesday's session saw the S&P 500 slip 0.15% to 738.18 as the Hormuz crisis entered its 10th week with WTI at $101.02. The April CPI report released Tuesday morning showed inflation remaining sticky, keeping the Fed on pause and the US10Y at 4.46%. Semis bore the brunt of the selling — SOXX plunged 3.2% to 515.99, erasing Monday's record, while BigTech held firmer with MSFT near flat and defending its 50-day average.
commodities_note: WTI held above $101 as the US-Iran stalemate persisted — Trump's swift rejection of Iran's latest peace offer on Monday dashed ceasefire hopes. Gold eased 0.1% to $4,704.60 after Monday's all-time high. QQQ data shows an anomaly: the $707.24 price is outside the expected $250–$700 range — verify the data source; the 5-day and 1-month percentage changes remain the reliable trend indicators.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks are now CAUTION — DBS and OCBC overbought above RSI 69, UOB downgraded from WATCH as RSI crossed the 65 hard block. US10Y climbed to 4.46%, above the 4.20% threshold where NIM relief begins, and the next MAS decision isn't until October 2026. The divergence within the cluster has collapsed — where OCBC previously led on YoY profit growth, all three now trade on the same macro headwind with no intra-cluster differentiation.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — dual NIM compression from US rates and S$NEER persists, no relief catalyst before October 2026 MAS review.
singapore.key_development: UOB downgraded to CAUTION from WATCH as RSI 68 triggered the hard block; all three banks now CAUTION for the first time since the NIM compression cycle began.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX plunged 3.2% to 515.99, erasing Monday's record, as all 14 cluster members remain CAUTION. The sell-off was broad — ASML -2.8%, MU -4.0%, LITE -6.4%, TSEM -4.7% — with only NVDA (+0.5%) bucking the trend ahead of its May 21 earnings. The cluster's uniform CAUTION posture reflects a sector-wide extension problem, not individual thesis breaks: 10 of 14 members are above the 20% extreme-extension threshold. The SOXX 5-day gain of 6.9% despite Tuesday's sell-off shows how much ground was covered in the prior rally.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically overextended — AI capex demand is confirmed but prices have overshot even the most optimistic revenue ramps.
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX -3.2% was the largest single-session drop in three weeks; TSEM reports today from a priced-for-perfection setup as the first test of whether earnings can justify the cluster's extreme extensions.

big_tech.summary: MSFT is the lone HOLD in a cluster otherwise deep in CAUTION — trading just 2.3% above its 50-day SMA with RSI 49.0 and R:R improving to 1.7:1. AMZN and GOOG remain 14-18% extended with no near-term pullback signal, while PLTR's technicals improved to -6.5% below SMA50 with RSI 43.9 — but the 66x forward PE fundamentals gate keeps it blocked. The cluster story is MSFT's near-actionable setup vs peers that are weeks-to-months away from entry. Azure's $50B quarter (per GeekWire) with non-OpenAI RPO at +28% provides the cleanest demand read in BigTech right now.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT near entry with improving R:R; AMZN/GOOG structurally extended; PLTR recovering technically but fundamentally gated.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's R:R improved to 1.7:1 as price pulled back toward the 50-day SMA at $398.73 — the nearest actionable entry in BigTech.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVDA photonics investment alongside LITE — the corroborating read-through on AI optics demand. NVDA's Blackwell Ultra ramp in Q2-Q3 2026 directly lifts the optics basket.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's pullback deepens below $850 and COHR shows correlated weakness below its own 50-day average.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and datacenter silicon competitor to AVGO and NVDA — benefits from the same hyperscaler capex cycle driving the Semis cluster. AVGO's June 4 earnings are a direct read-through for MRVL's custom-silicon pipeline.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AVGO's June 4 earnings for custom ASIC demand confirmation — MRVL's datacenter exposure makes it a second-order beneficiary.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking leader — NVDA Spectrum-X and 800G optical rollouts directly expand ANET's addressable market. MSFT Azure's $50B quarter signals sustained hyperscaler networking spend.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA's May 21 earnings confirm Blackwell Ultra volume ramp and networking attach rates exceed expectations.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz near-closure entering week 10 — Trump rejected Iran's latest peace proposal; OPEC output at lowest in two decades per Reuters survey (May 12). WTI holding above $101.

US-China tariff framework: 10% rate extended through November 2026 after May 2025 reduction; China maintains rare-earth export restrictions and 'unreliable entity' designations on US firms per Congress.gov CRS report (May 2026).
Middle East military escalation widening — airspace shut and ships halted across the region per SalmonBusiness (May 13); war-risk surcharges imposed by ocean carriers on Suez and Hormuz routes.
NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang joining Trump's China trip (per Reuters, May 13) — potential for new chip-export negotiations that could reshape the US-China semiconductor supply-chain dynamic.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $101.02 sustains inflation pressure that keeps the Fed on pause and the US10Y at 4.46% — directly extending the Singapore bank NIM compression cycle. Semis sold off 3.2% on Monday's SOXX record as the Iran stalemate added macro uncertainty atop extreme cluster extensions. Defense-adjacent names like PLTR remain the only geopolitical beneficiaries, but at 66x PE the premium is already priced in.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang confirmed to join Trump's China trade mission (per Reuters, May 13) — a potential diplomatic channel for semiconductor export negotiations.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries until either the Hormuz crisis de-escalates (WTI below $95) or individual tickers pull back to their 50-day SMAs — the combination of macro uncertainty and extreme cluster extensions leaves no clean setup.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Nineteen of 21 names are CAUTION — 10 on extreme extension above 20% of their 50-day SMAs. The Hormuz crisis at week 10 and US10Y at 4.46% have eliminated every near-term entry, leaving only MSFT and WRD as non-CAUTION names.
